# InboxCraft

> Generate PowerShell scripts to automate Outlook inbox rules — no code, no sign-up, nothing leaves your browser.

InboxCraft is a 100% client-side React + TypeScript app that takes sender addresses as input and instantly produces a ready-to-run, idempotent `.ps1` script that creates Outlook subfolders and Exchange Online inbox rules in a Microsoft 365 mailbox. No backend server exists; all processing happens in the browser.

## Key Concepts

- **Quick mode** — minimal input (M365 email + one sender), immediate `.ps1` download
- **Advanced mode** — bulk senders (one per line or comma-separated), custom parent folder, Copy or Move action, Exact or Contains match type, live folder-tree and rules-table preview
- **Templates Library** — pre-built YAML templates for popular services (GitHub, Slack, Teams, Jira, AWS, Salesforce, etc.); `?template=<id>` deep-link auto-opens the drawer
- **Domain-wide rules** — prefix a sender with `@` (e.g. `@slack.com`) to match an entire domain
- **Idempotent script** — checks `Get-InboxRule` and `Get-MailboxFolder` before creating; safe to re-run

## Generated Script Behaviour

The downloaded `outlook-rules.ps1` runs two steps:

1. **Folder creation** — via Outlook COM (no sign-in required, requires Outlook Desktop running); falls back to Exchange Online if COM is unavailable
2. **Rule creation** — via `New-InboxRule` (ExchangeOnlineManagement module); one rule per sender address pointing to its subfolder; skips rules that already exist by name

Prerequisites: PowerShell 5.1+, Microsoft 365 mailbox, ExchangeOnlineManagement module (script auto-installs if missing).

## Project Structure

- `src/App.tsx` — root component; all shared state lives here (no Context/Redux — intentional prop drilling)
- `src/pages/` — route-level pages: `HomePage`, `AdvancedPage`, `TemplatesPage`, `ChangelogPage`, `PrivacyPolicyPage`
- `src/components/` — UI primitives (`Button`, `Card`, `Badge`, `Textarea`, `EmailInput`, `CopyButton`) and composed widgets (`GeneratorOutput`, `ScriptOutput`, `RulesPreview`, `FolderTree`, `TemplateDrawer`)
- `src/hooks/` — custom React hooks for script generation logic
- `src/data/` — template data and static content
- `templates/` — plain YAML files, one per service; adding a new template requires no code changes
- `src/lib/` — PowerShell AST code generator that maps UI state to raw `.ps1` syntax

## Tech Stack

- React 18 + TypeScript, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS v3
- React Router v7 (HashRouter — deployed as a static GitHub Pages site)
- shiki for syntax-highlighted PowerShell output (poimandres theme)
- `yaml` package for parsing template files at runtime
- PostHog for anonymous, aggregated analytics (no form content captured)
- Changesets for versioning and changelog management

## Template YAML Schema

```yaml
id: my-service          # used in URLs (?template=my-service)
name: My Service
category: Collaboration # Collaboration | Dev Tools | CRM | Finance | Cloud | Productivity
description: One-line description shown on the card
folder: my-service      # Outlook subfolder name under the parent
emoji: "🚀"
updated: "2026-03-30"
senders:
  - noreply@my-service.com
  - "@my-service.com"   # domain-wide — quote lines starting with @
```

## Deployment

Static site deployed to GitHub Pages at `https://trivedi-vatsal.github.io/InboxCraft/`. HashRouter enables client-side routing without server configuration.
